title: lichen_l2.c:155 LICHEN_FRAME_MIN_MIC uses link.h constant but no include guard
description:
Completeness check: Round 1-2 missed header dependency issue.

WHAT: Line 155 uses LICHEN_MIC_32_LEN from link.h:
```c
#define LICHEN_FRAME_MIN_MIC      LICHEN_MIC_32_LEN  /* Use authoritative constant from link.h */
```

But link.h is included conditionally at line 73:
```c
#if defined(CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK)
#include <lichen/link.h>
```

WHY BUG: If CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK is not defined, the code would fail at line 155 because LICHEN_MIC_32_LEN is undefined. Currently this is protected by BUILD_ASSERT at line 94 which requires HAVE_LICHEN_LINK, but:
1. The BUILD_ASSERT fires with a message about 'raw IPv6-over-LoRa'
2. The actual compile error (undefined LICHEN_MIC_32_LEN) fires first
3. The error message doesn't explain the real issue

IMPACT: Confusing compile error if someone disables CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK. The BUILD_ASSERT should ideally be placed BEFORE any code that depends on link.h symbols.

FIX: Either:
1. Move the LICHEN_MIC_32_LEN usage inside #if HAVE_LICHEN_LINK guards
2. Provide a fallback definition: #ifndef LICHEN_MIC_32_LEN #define LICHEN_MIC_32_LEN 4 #endif
3. Reorder so BUILD_ASSERT(HAVE_LICHEN_LINK) appears before any link.h symbol usage
close_reason: Added an early CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK preprocessor guard before lichen_l2.c references link.h constants; verified lichen_l2.c object compile with forced L2/link config
